skill.name: url-citation-search
skill.description: Find papers and preprints that cite a specific URL, including blogs, documentation pages, project pages, and other web sources.
skill.summary: Search, verify, and report academic works that cite a web URL.
skill.author: zjsxply

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged markdown inline code spans as Ruby shell backticks and flagged the frontmatter description as weak cryptography. Manual review found no executable code, no scripts, no prompt injection, and no malicious intent. The skill only provides a research workflow for searching and verifying URL citations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are markdown inline code spans that name URL variants, source domains, metadata fields, and output labels. They are documentation text, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is markdown documentation and these backticks delimit inline text. No command runner, script file, shell invocation, or user input execution path exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Description
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to the skill description line. That line describes citation search behavior and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter prose for marketplace discovery. There is no code or semantic evidence of cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Web pages are often cited in papers but missed by standard citation indexes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through targeted searches, verification, and clear citation reports.
actual_capabilities: Find candidate papers that cite a supplied URL, title, slug, or mirror page.
Verify citations using PDF text, HTML references, metadata, or visible bibliography entries.
Separate direct citations, official URL variants, mirror citations, and unverified candidates.
Deduplicate preprints and published versions while keeping useful access links.
Produce brief lists, citation reports, or BibTeX for citing works.
limitations: It depends on search access, publisher availability, and readable PDF or HTML references.
It cannot prove that no citation exists across all private or unindexed sources.
Search snippets alone are not accepted as final evidence.
PDF extraction can miss text or link annotations and may require manual inspection.

use_cases.title: Trace Blog Impact
use_cases.description: Find papers that cite a technical blog post, project page, or documentation article.

use_cases.title: Prepare Evidence Reports
use_cases.description: Create a verified list of citing works with DOI, arXiv, evidence notes, and citation type.

use_cases.title: Check Moved URLs
use_cases.description: Compare canonical, migrated, mirrored, and same-site URL variants before reporting citation coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Basic URL Citation Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Find papers or preprints that cite this URL: [URL]. Return confirmed results only with evidence notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a verified citation list.

prompt_templates.title: Citation Report With Variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for citations to [URL]. Include canonical URL, title variants, mirrors, direct citations, official variants, and candidates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request when URL moves or mirrors may matter.

prompt_templates.title: Bibliography Evidence Review
prompt_templates.prompt: For each candidate citing work for [URL], inspect PDF or HTML references and explain the evidence quality.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced verification where search results are not enough.

prompt_templates.title: Deduplicated BibTeX Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Find confirmed citing works for [URL], deduplicate preprints and publications, then provide BibTeX for the citing papers.

output_examples.input: Find papers that cite a project documentation URL.
output: A confirmed citation report with paper titles, years, landing links, and evidence notes.
Each result is marked as direct, official variant, mirror, or candidate.

output_examples.input: Check whether an arXiv paper cites a moved blog post.
output: A short verification note explaining the searched URL variants and title variants.
The result states whether the bibliography contains the target, a mirror, or no verified citation.

best_practices: Provide the exact target URL and any known old domains or mirrors.
Ask for confirmed citations when you need reliable evidence.
Request BibTeX only after the citing works are verified and deduplicated.
anti_patterns: Do not treat search snippets as proof of citation.
Do not merge published and preprint records without checking both references.
Do not assume a topical paper cites the URL unless the reference is visible.

faq.question: What kind of URL can I search?
faq.answer: You can search blogs, documentation, project pages, demos, GitHub pages, and other citeable web pages.

faq.question: Does it use citation indexes only?
faq.answer: No. It also uses URL, title, slug, mirror, PDF, and HTML reference checks.

faq.question: Can it return BibTeX?
faq.answer: Yes. It can return BibTeX for verified citing papers when you request it.

faq.question: How does it handle mirrors?
faq.answer: It keeps mirror citations separate from direct citations and official same-site variants.

faq.question: Can it prove that no citation exists?
faq.answer: No. It can report no verified citation found after the selected search and verification steps.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found documentation only, with no executable code or confirmed dangerous behavior.
